name: on-chip-weights

interface_signals.name: data_in
interface_signals.role: one packed activation pixel per beat

interface_signals.name: data_out
interface_signals.role: one packed output pixel per beat

interface_signals.name: weight_rd_addr
interface_signals.role: URAM read address; layer's base address is parameter-loaded as weight_base_word

interface_signals.name: weight_rd_data
interface_signals.role: URAM-word-wide read data (288 bits on UltraScale+ URAM72; 36 INT8 weights per word)

interface_signals.name: weight_base_word
interface_signals.role: Layer's URAM base address (in 288-bit URAM words), loaded by scheduler before engine_start

fit_constraints.bram_formula: Weights live in a shared on-chip UltraRAM region whose base address is parameter-loaded per layer; on-chip storage is bounded by the URAM region budget (e.g. ZCU102 ~25 Mbit, U250 ~90 Mbit) and is NOT recomputed per layer. The active bias/scale ROMs use BRAM18 sized as OC*4 bytes (bias) + OC*4 bytes (scale_mult).
buffer_sizing_rules: Public streaming interface is identical to flat-bus (one packed activation pixel per beat).
Weight reads come from a URAM region whose base address is parameter-loaded per layer (weight_base_word).
URAM read data is a full URAM72 word (288 bits = 36 INT8 weights); address arithmetic must operate in URAM words, not bytes.
Activations are still streamed end-to-end; the activation BRAM ping-pong is managed by the scheduler outside this module.

protocol_rules: All weight bytes for this layer must be addressable as a contiguous URAM region starting at weight_base_word.
The module must not have any external memory interface (no AXI4-MM master, no DDR controller, no AXI read channel).
Weight reads must use weight_rd_en + weight_rd_addr + weight_rd_data; no $readmemh of the full weight tensor inside the module (the URAM is initialised at bitfile load by the top-level wrapper).
weight_base_word must be stable from at least one cycle before engine_start through the layer's final valid_out beat; the scheduler enforces this externally.
